channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Ukraine drone attack hits Russia’s Amazon warehouse
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A Ukrainian drone attack struck a Wildberries warehouse near Saint Petersburg, Russia, causing a fire and injuries. This is the third attack on Russia’s Am
Read More: https://punchng.com/ukraine-drone-attack-hits-russias-amazon-warehouse/

item.title: US imposes 12.5% tariff on Nigerian imports over forced labour claims
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The US has imposed a 12.5% tariff on Nigerian imports due to forced labor claims. This new trade measure affects 60 economies failing to ban forced labor g
Read More: https://punchng.com/us-imposes-12-5-tariff-on-nigerian-imports-over-forced-labour-claims/

item.title: United Nigeria suspends Benin flights after runway closure
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
United Nigeria Airlines has suspended flights to Benin Airport due to a runway closure by FAAN after an aircraft veered off. Passengers affected by the Ben
Read More: https://punchng.com/united-nigeria-suspends-benin-flights-after-runway-closure/

item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Nigerian Senate is advancing a bill to compel global social media companies like Facebook and TikTok to establish physical offices in Nigeria, aiming f
Read More: https://punchng.com/senate-pushes-bill-for-facebook-tiktok-offices-in-nigeria/

item.title: W’ Bank loans drive Tinubu’s social spending agenda
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu’s administration is increasingly relying on World Bank loans to fund social programs, aiming to ease reform pains and support vulnerable N

item.description:
President Tinubu urges Northeast governors to intensify efforts against terrorism and banditry, highlighting economic stability and infrastructure commitme

item.title: Today on Sky Sports Racing: Silver Dominion faces Alfred Wallace at Ascot
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: It's Silver Dominion versus Alfred Wallace as King George weekend gets under way at Ascot - live on Sky Sports Racing.

item.title: Stop Campaign Of Calumny Against INEC, Group Cautions Opposition
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Arewa Youth Forum, the umbrella body of youth organisations across the 19 Northern States and the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), has expressed support for the chairman of the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC), Prof. Joash Amupitan (SAN), and called on the opposition, civil socie

item.title: Disney’s ‘Moana’ hits milestone at West African box office
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Disney’s ‘Moana’ has recorded an impressive box office performances, highlighting the continued strong appetite for international films across West Africa. FilmOne Entertainment made this announcement via its Instagram page revealing that the animated adventure, ‘Moana’, grossed N61 million at the W

item.title: Barcelona’s new signing, Adeyemi names his two idols
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Barcelona’s new signing, Karim Adeyemi, has named Lionel Messi and Ronaldinho as his two idols at the club. Adeyemi stated this at his unveiling as a Barcelona player on Thursday. “For me, it’s a unique opportunity. My role models and idols at Barça are Messi and also Ronaldinho, al

item.title: Transfer: Man Utd reach agreement to sign France World Cup star for Carrick
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Manchester United have reached an agreement in principle with France World Cup star, Manu Kone. The AS Roma midfielder has been on the radar of several European clubs this summer transfer window. The 25-year-old gave an impressive performance for Les Bleus during the 2026 FIFA World Cup tournament.

item.title: 2027: PFN kicks against Muslim-Muslim ticket
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "Nigeria is a multi-religious and multi-ethnic nation whose diversity should be reflected in its leadershi" The post 2027: PFN kicks against Muslim-Muslim ticket appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Firm, Retired General Clash Over Land Ownership Despite Ongoing Police Probe
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A fresh land dispute in the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) has taken a dramatic turn after a firm, Manec Homes Limited, clashed with a retired Major General over the ownership of Plot No. 446 in Cadastral Zone C01, Karmo District, Abuja. The firm accused the Army General of forcefully taking over t

item.title: PHOTOS: First Lady Remi Tinubu’s visit to Abia to promote Akwete weaving heritage
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Mrs Tinubu donated N2 billion to the Abia State Government to support the development of Akwete fabric, a prestigious hand-woven textile indigenous to the Akwete community in Abia. The post PHOTOS: First Lady Remi Tinubu’s visit to Abia to promote Akwete weaving heritage appeared first on Premium Ti

item.title: Ballon d’Or 2026: I see him picking it – Agbonlahor names player to win award
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Aston Villa captain, Gabby Agbonlahor, has tipped Spain winger, Lamine Yamal to win the Ballon d’Or ahead of the likes of England’s Harry Kane, France’s Kylian Mbappe, Argentina’s Lionel Messi and Georgia’s Kvicha Kvaratskhelia. The former England international s

item.title: U.S. announces new global visa restrictions targeting cybercriminals, family members
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The United States has introduced a new global visa restriction policy targeting individuals involved in cybercrime, cyberscams and sextortion, as the Trump administration steps up efforts to combat online fraud and transnational criminal networks. The post U.S. announces new global visa restrictions

item.title: ‘You privatized government assets, left pensioners unpaid’ – Tinubu knocks Atiku
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has blasted the African Democratic Congress, ADC, presidential candidate, Atiku Abubakar for privatizing government assets and leaving pensioners unpaid. Tinubu said this on Thursday during his meeting with Northeast stakeholders who paid him a thank-you visit for re-

item.title: Kebbi Gov’s Wife Distributes Therapeutic Food To Hospitals
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Wife of the Kebbi State governor, Dr Nafisa Nasir Idris, has flagged off the distribution of Ready-to- Use Therapeutic Food (RUTF)/Ton Brown Nutritionist Commodities to some hospitals in the state. The programme is being implemented by the Kebbi State government in collaboration with the National Co

item.title: Appeal court vacates order freezing 124 bank accounts ‘linked to’ Aisha Achimugu
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Court of Appeal sitting in Port Harcourt, Rivers State, has vacated the interim order freezing 124 bank accounts belonging to businesswoman, Aisha Achimugu and companies linked to her, ruling that allowing the ex parte order to remain in force for over 15 months amounted to an abuse of court pro

item.title: Death Toll Rises To 23 As Bandits Attack Zamfara Farmers
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The death toll from a daylight attack by bandits on farmlands in Sauna District, Talata Mafara local government area of Zamfara State has risen to 23, community sources confirmed yesterday. Earlier reports put the number of those killed at 16. According to fresh accounts from the Sauna District, the

item.title: Transfer: Chelsea to announce deal for Argentina World Cup star, Valentin Barco
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Chelsea’s summer recruitment efforts are gaining momentum, with Argentina’s World Cup star, Valentin Barco poised to be the latest signing. Xabi Alonso is eager to revamp a squad that failed to secure European qualification after another tumultuous season, which saw Enzo Maresca leave in

item.title: Oriire Abduction: Court Jails 3 Ansaru Terrorists For Life
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Justice Salim Ibrahim of the Federal High Court, Abuja, has sentenced three persons to life imprisonment over their complicity in the kidnapping of students and teachers of a school in Orire local government area (LGA) of Oyo State. The court convicted and sentenced the defendants, Abdulrazak Umar (

item.title: Benin Airport closure triggers flight cancellations by United Nigeria Airlines
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: "We wish to inform our valued passengers and the general public that, due to the closure of the runway by the Federal Airport Authority of Nigeria (FAAN) as a result of an excursion, all our flights to and from Benin Airport have been cancelled pending its reopening."

item.title: EFCC hands over recovered N108m to Lagos company
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Investigation revealed that the suspects, Nkechi Gladys Megai, who is currently at large after fleeing to the United Kingdom, and Godson Eze, a former Chief Financial Officer, abused their positions by forging documents, creating fake invoices in the names of the company's legitimate vendors, and di

item.title: Plumptre Signs New Two-Year Deal With Al-Ittihad
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Super Falcons defender Ashleigh Plumptre has signed a new two-year contract with Saudi Women’s Premier League club Al-Ittihad Ladies, extending her stay until 2028. Plumptre committed her future to the Jeddah-based side after establishing herself as one of the club’s key performers since

item.title: United Nigeria Airlines cancels Benin flights pending runway reopening
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: United Nigeria Airlines has temporarily halted all scheduled flights to and from Benin Airport after the Federal Airports Authority of Nigeria (FAAN) shut the airport's runway citing safety concerns after an aircraft runway excursion. The post United Nigeria Airlines cancels Benin flights pending ru

item.title: US new visa ban targets cyberscammers, sextortionists
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The United States has implemented a new global visa ban to deter and combat internet crime, fraud, and predatory schemes against its nationals. Secretary of State, Marco Rubio announced the restriction policy on Thursday, citing the “unprecedented threat

item.title: Who am I? Guess Premier League star No 4
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Clarify Results Transmission Before 2027, Osun Gov’ship Polls, Yiaga Africa Tells INEC
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A civil society organisation, Yiaga Africa, has charged the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) to promptly publish a clear protocol on electronic transmission of election results ahead of the 15 th August Osun governorship election and the 2027 general election. The group said the prot

item.title: Osun poll: Accord Party, Police differ over officers’ neutrality
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Police dismissed the allegations, describing them as false and misleading, maintaining that it remained committed to professionalism The post Osun poll: Accord Party, Police differ over officers’ neutrality appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Discipline over talent – Ramsey Nouah speaks on career sustenance
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: He noted that despite his age and long run in the movie industry, he remains relevant because of his discipline and approach to the craft.

item.title: I Will Do One Term In Office, Says ADC Gov’ship Candidate
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The governorship candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) in Plateau State, Brig-Gen John Sura (rtd), says if elected into office, he would serve one term to avoid truncating the state’s zoning arrangements. Sura said any focused leader such as himself does not require a lengthy peri

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Good morning to readers. Daily Sun brings you the major stories from Nigerian Newspapers on Friday morning. The post Nigerian Newspapers: Major stories on Friday morning appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Why Rogers and Palmer will thrive together in Alonso's Chelsea
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Why Morgan Rogers' skillset means he and fellow attacking midfielder Cole Palmer will prosper in Xabi Alonso's Chelsea.

item.title: Woman Dies After Giving Birth To Triplets In Kebbi
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A housewife (name withheld) in Yangar Hausawa under Etene ward of Kalgo local government area of Kebbi State has died soon after she delivered three babies. A relative of the deceased, Malama Saratu Labaran, who disclosed the incident to journalists in Birnin Kebbi yesterday, lamented that the babie

item.title: Obasanjo to Osoba: ‘I didn’t deceive AD leaders in 2003’
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Obasanjo said developments preceding his emergence as President were unknown to Osoba because they were handled by senior Afenifere and AD leaders The post Obasanjo to Osoba: ‘I didn’t deceive AD leaders in 2003’ appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: ‘They must be held accountable’ – US govt condemns killing of Dachomo’s family members
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The United States government has condemned the killing of family members of Rev. Ezekiel Dachomo in Plateau State, describing the attack as horrific and calling for those responsible to be brought to justice. In a statement posted on its official Facebook page on Wednesday, the US Mission in Ni

item.title: Task Before The New NBA President
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya, SAN, has made history as the first elected female president of the Nigerian Bar Association, polling 12,317 votes, or 47.18 per cent of the 26,106 ballots cast, to defeat two male challengers. She is only the second woman to lead the association in its 66-year existence,

item.title: Obasanjo Replies Osoba, Denies Betraying AD Leaders’ Trust In 2023 Polls
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former President Olusegun Obasanjo on Thursday denied the allegation of deceitful romance and betrayal of the trust of the leaders of the Alliance for Democracy (AD) in the build-up to the 2003 general elections, levelled against him by former governor of Ogun State, Aremo Olusegun Osoba. Obasanjo,

item.title: Preseason Friendly: Super Eagles Defender Reflects On Blackburn’s Loss Vs Nottingham Forest
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Super Eagles defender Ryan Alebiosu has disclosed that regaining match fitness and getting valuable playing time were the main positives following Blackburn Rovers’s 3-0 loss to Nottingham Forest in a preseason friendly. In a chat with Rovers TV, the Nigerian international noted that matching

item.title: World Cup: Football integrity body flags 15 matches with potential betting issues
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Group of Copenhagen, an organization dedicated to football integrity, has identified several incidents as potential spot-betting concerns during the World Cup.  Although the complete report has not yet been released to the public, these irregularities are currently receiving significant att

item.title: Ballon d’Or: Barcelona’s new signing, Adeyemi names favourite to win award
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Barcelona’s newest addition, Karim Adeyemi, has said that there is no reason Lamine Yamal should not be awarded the Ballon d’Or this season. He made this statement while discussing the potential for Yamal to reach the level of Lionel Messi, amidst ongoing comparisons between the two La M

item.title: Ukraine beheads its military as its performance begins to soar
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: An unprecedented simultaneous replacement of the top defence and military brass comes amid a debate over strategy.

item.title: China Rushes to Buy East Russian Oil as Middle East Risks Grow
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Chinese buyers are snapping up Russia’s flagship ESPO crude weeks earlier than usual, as heightened risks to Middle East oil flows prompt refiners to secure supplies before they’re needed.

item.title: UK Pub Stocks Fall as Heat Wave Proves Too Hot For Football Fans
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: UK pub stocks slumped this week as a sales boost from the World Cup failed to offset the drag from a brutal heat wave and higher costs.

item.title: Ecuadorian Richard Carapaz wins Stage 18 in a solo breakaway
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Following a decisive attack in the closing stages, Richard Carapaz claimed victory on Stage 18, leaving all his rivals trailing in his wake. He finished ahead of Switzerland’s Mauro Schmid and the American Matteo Jorgenson, who crossed the line 45 seconds behind him. Today, the riders face another m

item.title: World’s largest Ahmadiyya Islamic convention begins today in UK, over 50 Nigerians participate

item.title: Enugu Air plane skids off runway at Benin airport
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: An aircraft operated by Enugu Air on Thursday skidded off the runway of the Benin airport during landing on Thursday.

item.title: Uganda: MPs Receive Shs300 Million Car Grant, Want More. . .
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Nile Post] Members of Parliament on Thursday received Shs300 million each as a car grant, but the payment has triggered questions within the House after legislators noted that the amount was lower than what had been approved.

item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Premium Times] The three convicts were jailed for six offences, and they will stand trial on the four remaining charges to which they pleaded not guilty.

item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [New Times] American billionaire Bill Gates visited Rwanda to explore the country's progress in health, artificial intelligence and digital public infrastructure.

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: A $1.15 trillion US defence bill passed by the House includes plans to expand military cooperation with Israel.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Udeme Akpan, Energy Editor Nigeria may be heading for another round of inflationary pressure as crude oil prices climb above $100 per barrel following the escalating conflict between the United States and Iran, raising fears of higher petrol prices, increased transport fares and cost of living. H

item.title: NCAA threatens Royal Air Maroc with suspension over service breaches
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Nigeria’s civil aviation regulator has issued a direct threat to suspend the operational permit of Moroccan flag carrier Royal Air

item.title: Ethiopia: Armed attacks in East Arsi and the need for immediate attention
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Ethiopia is one of the oldest nations in the world with a rich history of resilience and cultural diversity, which has led to complex ethnic dynamics among numerous groups, including misconceptions about the Amhara people.

item.title: Longlist for the 2026 Nigeria Prize for Literature Announced
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Sponsored by Nigeria LNG Limited (NLNG), the Nigeria Prize for Literature is regarded as Africa's richest literary prize and one of the most prestigious in the world. The award rotates annually among four genres—prose fiction, poetry, drama and children's literature—with this year's edition dedicate

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: South Africa’s central bank unexpectedly left its benchmark interest rate unchanged on Thursday, defying market expectations for another increase as

item.title: Dangote Prefers London Over Dubai for Listing of Cement Unit
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Dangote Cement Plc prefers to list its cement business in London because it will be quicker to sell shares on the exchange in the UK than in Dubai, according to Mariya Dangote, a board member in the cement business.

item.title: Global Bonds Are Reeling as Oil Surge Renews Inflation Threat
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Global bonds are being pummeled by the latest resurgence in energy prices, delivering losses to investors who bet the worst of this year’s rout was over and teeing up credibility tests for central bankers.

item.title: Albanian police use water cannon on protesters over Kushner-linked resort

item.description: Police used water cannons and pepper spray as protesters clashed outside Albania’s parliament.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Nigerian Safety Investigation Bureau (NSIB) has commenced investigation into a runway excursion involving an Enugu Air Embraer E170 aircraft at Benin Airport, Edo State. The post Enugu Air plane skids off runway with 68 onboard appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.description: The tariffs target 60 trading partners over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour.

item.description: • Directs immediate compliance with committee invitations, directs SGF, ministers to enforce resolution • As Reps warn NRS chairman Zacch Adedeji of sanctions over recruitment, recalcitrance Sunday Aborisade and Juliet

item.title: Senate Pushes Bigger FERMA Budget, Orders Emergency Rescue of Failed Federal Roads
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: • Approves N15.06bn intervention on Benue road, warns Works Ministry to prioritise rehabilitation over new projects Sunday Aborisade in Abuja The Senate on Thursday mounted fresh pressure on the federal

item.title: At Bello’s Book Launch, Amupitan Seeks INEC’s Independence, Seamless Operations
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: • Insists commission needs financial autonomy for free, fair, credible elections  •Shettima: trust grows when citizens feel seen, heard, valued, and protected.d  •Says Fourth Republic has been long conversation between

item.title: Shettima: Africa Must Play More Prominent Role in Shaping Global Digital Agenda
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: • ATU partners GSMA to raise Africa’s telecom revenue base Oghenevwede Ohwovoriole in Abuja Vice President Kashim Shettima has stated that Africa must play a more prominent role in shaping

item.title: Tuggar Campaign Refutes Allegiance to Abubakar, Denounces Outcome of Bauchi APC Guber Primary
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Emmanuel Addeh in Abuja The campaign organisation of Ambassador Yusuf Tuggar Thursday refuted a report that supporters of the erstwhile Minister of Foreign Affairs had decided to back the All

item.title: Heroic rescue, but are we ready to tame kidnapping?
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: NIGERIA breathed a collective sigh of relief when news broke that the abducted schoolchildren and teachers in Oyo State had regained their freedom. The post Heroic rescue, but are we ready to tame kidnapping? appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Communal farming: Unity against hunger
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THERE was a time when cooperation and food security were the cornerstones of farming. It was a way of life I once lived, witnessed and participated in as a child during school vacations in Okopedi, Okobo Local Government Area of Akwa Ibom The post Communal farming: Unity against hunger appeared fir

item.title: As oil soars, experts watch Red Sea tankers for clarity on Houthi blockade
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Houthi attacks for now are shaping who moves Saudi crude, not whether it moves, analysts say, even as oil prices soar.

item.title: Korean Stocks Sink as Chipmakers Drop on Middle East, China IPO
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: South Korean stocks fell as funds cut exposure to chipmakers after overnight losses in US peers, while factoring in a Chinese rival’s imminent share sale and rising Middle East tensions.

item.title: Metals Fluctuate as Middle East Escalation Keep Bulls in Check
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Base metals fluctuated at the end of the week as the threat of an escalating conflict in the Middle East — and more turbulence for the global economy — kept a lid on optimism.

item.title: Cartoon
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: By D.D. Onu The post Cartoon appeared first on The Guardian Nigeria News - Nigeria and World News.

item.title: What are the Abraham Accords?
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US President Donald Trump has seemingly backpedalled on a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia.
